title: Scalus 2026: Maintenance, Dijkstra Readiness, Interoperability & Application Runtime

rationale.summary: Vote: YES. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), available evidence supports proceeding, with risk judged manageable under current context. Decisive reason: the proposal substantiates "Scalus is an open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery." and clears the evidence gates. Weighed against it: a material claim is unsupported — "Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm projects." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. What could still change this vote to NO: Material counter-evidence on the decisive points above, or failure of the stated safeguards, would trigger a revision. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score 0.26; confidence 78.5%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was +0.020 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: A small positive nudge is justified because the claims describe maintenance of existing open-source infrastructure, a narrowed scope versus the prior proposal, no contingency, milestone delivery, and concrete technical deliverables tied to protocol readiness. The nudge remains small because important adoption and oversight claims are mostly asserted in the document rather than evidenced there, and treasury stewardship still requires caution on a large withdrawal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm projects.
Provide independent evidence for: Scalus components are reused in MeshJS SDK, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Funding for a 9-month continuation of Scalus covering maintenance, Dijkstra hard-fork readiness, interoperability across JVM and JS/TS ecosystems, and a scoped application runtime.

Deliverables: Ongoing maintenance including bug fixes, dependency upkeep, security patches, and releases, Dijkstra readiness including Plutus V4 support, ledger rules, transaction-builder updates, cost models, nested transactions, accounts, guard scripts, and conformance testing, Improved JS/TS interfaces and documentation for Scalus components embedded in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, and Lucid Evolution, Java/Kotlin integration examples and cleaner integration paths with Cardano Client Lib and Yaci, Scoped runtime components including reactive workers, chain follower, task scheduler, backend workflows, and reference validation
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Scalus is an open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal asks for ₳2,464,844 over 9 months at a $0.16/ADA reference rate with no contingency.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm projects.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Scalus components are reused in MeshJS SDK, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposer reports three years of development, 4,642 commits, 32 releases, 12 contributors, and 20,000+ monthly Scalus.js downloads.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): This resubmission reduces the prior proposal from ₳8.503M over 12 months to about ₳2.465M over 9 months and narrows scope by removing the standalone L1 node, full L2 integration, broad formal verification, and advanced devnet expansion.
missing: Independent evidence for: Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm projects.
Independent evidence for: Scalus components are reused in MeshJS SDK, Evolution SDK, Lucid Evolution, Cardano Client Lib, and YaciDevKit.
Independent evidence for: Delivery is described as milestone-based and administered through audited SundaeSwap treasury contracts with an independent oversight board and third-party assurance.
Independent evidence for: Lantr Engineering says it will commit to public reporting, open community sessions, and on-time milestone delivery for treasury-funded work.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Scalus is an open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Scalus is used directly by Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm projects." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.merit_reason: Intrinsic benefit, delivery, cost, and downside-risk floors all pass.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.

rationale.merit_reason: Intrinsic benefit, delivery, cost, and downside-risk floors all pass.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
